name: Sai Baba Samadhi Mandir
description: The main shrine of Shirdi Sai Baba containing his samadhi (tomb) and a beautiful marble idol. Queue darshan runs 24/7 during festivals.

name: Dwarkamai Mosque
description: The dilapidated mosque where Sai Baba lived for 60 years. Contains the sacred dhuni (fire) he kept burning and the grinding stone.

name: Chavadi
description: Sai Baba would sleep here on alternate nights. Every Thursday a procession re-enacts his walk from Dwarkamai to Chavadi.

name: Shani Shingnapur
description: Famous temple of Lord Shani (Saturn) where the deity is a self-emerged black stone. The village has no doors — theft is considered impossible.

name: Gurusthan
description: The spot where Sai Baba was first seen as a young boy sitting under a neem tree in deep meditation. A sacred neem tree still stands.

name: Lendi Baug
description: Garden where Sai Baba would take daily walks and water the plants himself. Contains a lamp he lit that is still burning today.

name: Dixit Wada Museum
description: Museum with personal belongings of Sai Baba — his kafni, wooden sandals, tumbler, and photographs. Built by devotee Hari Sitaram Dixit.

name: Khandoba Temple
description: Temple where a priest named Mhalsapati first greeted Sai Baba with 'Aao Sai' (Welcome, Sai). Historical starting point of the Sai legend.

name: Sai Tirth Devotional Theme Park
description: Theme park depicting Sai Baba's life through wax figures, 7D shows, and interactive exhibits. Good for families and children.

name: Sai Heritage Village
description: Recreated village showing the 19th-century Shirdi that Sai Baba lived in. Life-size models of the original Dwarkamai, Chavadi, and village.

name: Trimbakeshwar Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas near Nashik. Unique three-faced lingam representing Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva. Source of the Godavari river.

name: Bhimashankar Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas nestled in the Sahyadri ranges. Surrounded by dense forests and home to the Indian giant squirrel.

name: Sakuri Ashram
description: Ashram of Upasni Maharaj, a direct disciple of Sai Baba. Peaceful spiritual retreat with beautiful architecture and gardens.

name: Nashik Panchavati
description: Sacred area where Lord Rama lived during exile. Contains Ram Kund, Sita Gufa, and Kalaram Temple — major Hindu pilgrimage sites.

name: Shirdi Nagar Darshan
description: Walking tour through the lanes Sai Baba walked daily — from Dwarkamai to Lendi Baug route. Locals share oral history of the saint.

name: Saibaba Sansthan Trust
description: Administrative trust managing all Shirdi temples. Also runs free meals (anna prasad) feeding 50,000+ devotees daily.

name: Changdev Maharaj Temple
description: Temple of the ancient yogi Changdev who is said to have been 1400 years old when he met Sant Dnyaneshwar. Peaceful hilltop location.

name: Naldurg Fort
description: Massive Bahmani-era fort with India's only fort-based waterfall. During monsoon, the moat overflows creating a stunning cascade.

name: Wet N Joy Waterpark
description: Large waterpark near Shirdi. Good break for families visiting with children. Wave pool, slides, and rain dance areas.

name: Pandav Leni Caves
description: 24 rock-cut Buddhist caves near Nashik dating to 1st century BC. Fine Hinayana Buddhist sculptures and inscriptions.
